# UC-22.5.18 · MiFID II Market Abuse — Layering and Spoofing Pattern Scores by Trader

## Description

MAR surveillance expectations extend to member firms’ own algos and desks. Scoring rapid cancel-to-fill ratios and quote flicker rates supports detection of manipulative patterns for SMF16 oversight.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for MiFID II compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes trading and sourcetypes oms:order_lifecycle. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Calibrate c2f by instrument liquidity; (2) exclude market-making agreements via lookup; (3) integrate with STOR workflow; (4) strict data segregation; (5) model governance sign-off. Indexes required: `index=trading`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=oms:order_lifecycle`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Market data + OMS via HEC, or vendor surveillance feed.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=trading` `sourcetype="oms:order_lifecycle"` (order_id, trader_id, symbol, event, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Calibrate c2f by instrument liquidity; (2) exclude market-making agreements via lookup; (3) integrate with STOR workflow; (4) strict data segregation; (5) model governance sign-off.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=trading sourcetype="oms:order_lifecycle" earliest=-1d
| eval is_cancel=if(event="cancel",1,0), is_fill=if(event="fill",1,0)
| stats sum(is_cancel) as cancels, sum(is_fill) as fills by trader_id, symbol
| eval c2f=if(fills>0, cancels/fills, cancels)
| where c2f>50 AND fills>10
| sort - c2f
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Market Abuse — Layering and Spoofing Pattern Scores by Trader** — MAR surveillance expectations extend to member firms’ own algos and desks. Scoring rapid cancel-to-fill ratios and quote flicker rates supports detection of manipulative patterns for SMF16 oversight.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=trading` `sourcetype="oms:order_lifecycle"` (order_id, trader_id, symbol, event, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Market data + OMS via HEC, or vendor surveillance feed. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: trading; **sourcetype**: oms:order_lifecycle. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=trading, sourcetype="oms:order_lifecycle", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **is_cancel** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by trader_id, symbol** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **c2f** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where c2f>50 AND fills>10` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (scores), Scatter (cancels vs fills), Single value (traders flagged).

## SPL

```spl
index=trading sourcetype="oms:order_lifecycle" earliest=-1d
| eval is_cancel=if(event="cancel",1,0), is_fill=if(event="fill",1,0)
| stats sum(is_cancel) as cancels, sum(is_fill) as fills by trader_id, symbol
| eval c2f=if(fills>0, cancels/fills, cancels)
| where c2f>50 AND fills>10
| sort - c2f
```

## Visualization

Table (scores), Scatter (cancels vs fills), Single value (traders flagged).
